fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer occupational therapist license to idaho, occupational therapist compact idaho, idaho occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Idaho DOPL — Occupational Therapy Licensure Board
board.parent_dept: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL)
board.mailing_address:
Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses
P.O. Box 83720
Boise, ID 83720-0063

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT)
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education per renewal cycle

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Idaho.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create Account and Initiate Application
steps.description: Access the Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses Online Application Center, select 'Occupational Therapist,' and proceed to the Licensing Portal. Register for a new account or log in, then select the appropriate application type (e.g., 'Occupational Therapist by Examination' or 'Occupational Therapist by Endorsement') and complete all sections of the online application.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official academic transcripts, verifying your occupational therapy degree, to be sent directly from your educational institution to the Idaho Board of Occupational Therapists.

steps.title: Provide NBCOT Verification
steps.description: Ensure your certification by the National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) is active and request NBCOT to submit official verification of your certification directly to the Idaho Board.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the Licensing Portal or on the board's website to complete the fingerprinting process for a state and federal criminal background check.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the required non-refundable application fee through the Licensing Portal using a credit or debit card.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Idaho OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Idaho have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Idaho has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Idaho
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Idaho, you must navigate to the Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses Online Application Center, select 'Occupational Therapist,' and begin your application through their online Licensing Portal.

variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to the Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses Online Application Center.
2. Select 'Occupational Therapist' from the list of professions.
3. Click 'Start your Application,' which will redirect you to the Licensing Portal.
4. Click 'Register' to create a new user account if you don't have one, or log in if you do.
5. Follow the prompts to complete your registration and begin your online application.
